steps.title: Establish the confidence interval from margin of error
steps.content: The survey estimates 35% support with a margin of error of 3%. This means the true percentage supporting the legislation is plausibly between 35% − 3% = 32% and 35% + 3% = 38%.

steps.title: Convert percentage bounds to population counts
steps.content:
The population is 50,000 people. Apply the percentage bounds:
Lower bound: 0.32 × 50,000 = 16,000
Upper bound: 0.38 × 50,000 = 19,000
So a plausible value for the number of supporters falls between 16,000 and 19,000 people.

steps.title: Check which choice falls in the plausible range
steps.content:
Test each option:
  • A) 350 → represents 0.7% of the population, far too small
  • B) 650 → represents 1.3% of the population, far too small
  • C) 16,750 → represents 33.5% of 50,000, which lies within [32%, 38%]
  • D) 31,750 → represents 63.5% of the population, far too large
That matches choice C — C is correct.
